title: feat: add toggle for showing/hiding silkscreen in the viewer
description: Adds support for toggling the visibility of silkscreen layers in the PCB viewer, allowing users to show or hide silkscreen layers using a new checkbox in the view settings menu.
body:
This pull request adds support for toggling the visibility of silkscreen layers in the PCB viewer. Users can now show or hide silkscreen layers using a new checkbox in the view settings menu. The implementation includes updates to the global store, UI components, and rendering logic to handle this new setting.

https://linear.app/tscircuit/issue/TSC-437/toggle-for-show-silkscreen-in-pcb-viewer

**Silkscreen Layer Visibility Toggle:**

* Added a new `is_showing_silkscreen` property and corresponding setter (`setIsShowingSilkscreen`) to the global store, including local storage integration for persistence. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f5e9e6445dcb59006bbeb6339ce87b9f745367769903b5ae1ef8a16d35efb45dR37) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f5e9e6445dcb59006bbeb6339ce87b9f745367769903b5ae1ef8a16d35efb45dR57) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-f5e9e6445dcb59006bbeb6339ce87b9f745367769903b5ae1ef8a16d35efb45dR112-R115) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-f5e9e6445dcb59006bbeb6339ce87b9f745367769903b5ae1ef8a16d35efb45dR177-R180) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-071f3fc0d0995fbdbf5d2a49a57c01b41e3780b39e883c66a9d27a5f30ac8b2dR11)
* Updated the `ToolbarOverlay` component to include a "Show Silkscreen" checkbox in the view settings menu, wired to the new global store state and setter. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-2884ae32d92740f5f4934900becc1d4193fe4911bff5ee246038e2a85d24bdc3R248) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-2884ae32d92740f5f4934900becc1d4193fe4911bff5ee246038e2a85d24bdc3R271) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-2884ae32d92740f5f4934900becc1d4193fe4911bff5ee246038e2a85d24bdc3R285) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-2884ae32d92740f5f4934900becc1d4193fe4911bff5ee246038e2a85d24bdc3R842-R848)
* Modified `CanvasPrimitiveRenderer` to filter out silkscreen primitives and layers when silkscreen is disabled, and to only draw silkscreen elements when enabled. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09R80) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09L100-R105) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09L290-R293) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09L301) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09R313) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09R441) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-e8e0a7689a44afbe10abbff3361f06753715b8c8e32a98e5ac8af4a346408d09L461-R466)

These changes ensure that users have fine-grained control over silkscreen visibility, improving the flexibility and usability of the PCB viewer.

title: Add `is_filled` to `pcb_silkscreen_circle`
description: Adds an optional is_filled property to the pcb_silkscreen_circle Zod schema and TypeScript interface to indicate filled circles in PCB silkscreen data.
body:
### Motivation
- Make `pcb_silkscreen_circle` consistent with other silkscreen shapes by allowing an optional `is_filled` property so consumers can indicate filled circles in PCB silkscreen data.

### Description
- Added `is_filled: z.boolean().optional()` to the `pcb_silkscreen_circle` Zod schema in `src/pcb/pcb_silkscreen_circle.ts` to accept the property at runtime.
- Added `is_filled?: boolean` to the `PcbSilkscreenCircle` TypeScript interface so static types match the schema.

### Testing
- Ran `npm run build` and the project built successfully (ESM and DTS outputs generated).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69babc86ffdc83279083c84c14312946)

title: Add support for filled PCB silkscreen circles (fill attribute)
description: Enables rendering of filled silkscreen circles when the PCB data includes an is_filled flag, allowing circles to be filled with the layer color instead of being only stroked.
body:
### Motivation

- Enable rendering of filled silkscreen circles when the PCB data includes an `is_filled` flag so circles can be filled with the layer color instead of being only stroked.

### Description

- Update `createSvgObjectsFromPcbSilkscreenCircle` to read the `is_filled` property and set the SVG `fill` attribute to the layer color when `is_filled` is true and to `none` otherwise.
- Remove an unused import (`matrixToString`) from the transformation import list.
- Add a test case for a filled silkscreen circle and update the SVG snapshot to include explicit `fill` attributes on silkscreen circles.

### Testing

- Ran the unit test file `tests/pcb/pcb-silkscreen-circle.test.ts` which includes a new filled circle case and updated snapshot `tests/pcb/__snapshots__/pcb-silkscreen-circle.snap.svg`, and the tests passed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69bab9dfc5cc8327b6b2399778fb2fb7)

title: feat: add support for exporting kicad_pro format in exportSnippet and tests
description: Adds support for exporting KiCad project files (.kicad_pro) alongside schematic (.kicad_sch) and PCB (.kicad_pcb) files when exporting a circuit as a KiCad zip archive, ensuring the generated zip includes all three file types and updates the test suite accordingly.
body:
This pull request adds support for exporting KiCad project files (`.kicad_pro`) alongside schematic (`.kicad_sch`) and PCB (`.kicad_pcb`) files when exporting a circuit as a KiCad zip archive. The changes ensure that the generated zip includes all three file types and updates the test suite accordingly.

**KiCad project export enhancements:**

* Added `CircuitJsonToKicadProConverter` to the imports and used it to generate a `.kicad_pro` file during export, including it in the output zip archive. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f94ad8fccfce0ee8f63cbf7d7dbeb8a90f3b3b3e98de73ff4b60899a38f58791R10) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f94ad8fccfce0ee8f63cbf7d7dbeb8a90f3b3b3e98de73ff4b60899a38f58791R210-R219)

**Testing updates:**

* Updated the KiCad export test to check for the presence and content of the `.kicad_pro` file in the generated zip archive.

title: Prevent NaN in node failure probability calculation
description: Adds defensive handling around capacity calculations to avoid 00 and non-finite values in calculateNodeProbabilityOfFailure, while keeping the final NaN throw intact.
body: This change adds defensive handling around capacity calculations to avoid 0/0 and non‑finite values in calculateNodeProbabilityOfFailure, while keeping the final NaN throw intact. It also hardens getTunedTotalCapacity1 against invalid dimensions that would otherwise lead to NaN/Infinity.

title: Add blank hypergraph builder and SVG-based tests
description: Add createBlankHyperGraphFromHyperGraphWithSolvedRoutes to deserialize solved routes, strip synthetic boundary regions, and rebuild a blank serialized graph with new connection endpoints derived from port geometry; expand section-solver fixturestests to cover both section extraction and the blank-hypergraph conversion, plus add the corresponding fixture snapshot; refresh documentation and CI workflows to align with the new utilities and test suite.
body:
Summary
- add `createBlankHyperGraphFromHyperGraphWithSolvedRoutes` to deserialize solved routes, strip synthetic boundary regions, and rebuild a blank serialized graph with new connection endpoints derived from port geometry
- expand section-solver fixtures/tests (stacked SVG snapshots via `stack-svgs` and `toMatchSvgSnapshot`) to cover both section extraction and the blank-hypergraph conversion, plus add the corresponding fixture snapshot
- refresh documentation and CI workflows to align with the new utilities and test suite

Testing
- Not run (not requested)

title: Add extractSectionOfHyperGraph helper and snapshot test

body:
Summary
- add serialization helpers for solved routes, expose extractSectionOfHyperGraph, and update pipeline naming to use “deserializing” terminology
- include mutual network IDs during connection (de)serialization and wire solved-route reconstruction to round-trip section extraction
- cover the new behavior with a stacked SVG snapshot using the stack-svgs module so the full graph and section renderings are validated via `toMatchSvgSnapshot`

Testing
- Not run (not requested)

title: fix: use LayerRef for via route point from_layer/to_layer
description: Changes the from_layer and to_layer fields in the PcbTraceRoutePointVia interface from string to LayerRef, ensuring consistency with the wire route point variant and eliminating type assertions in downstream consumers.
body:
## Summary

- Change `PcbTraceRoutePointVia.from_layer` and `.to_layer` from `z.string()` to `layer_ref` in both the Zod schema and the TypeScript interface
- The wire route point variant already uses `LayerRef` for its `layer` field — this makes the via variant consistent
- Eliminates the need for `as LayerRef` type assertions in downstream consumers (e.g. dsn-converter)

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes (no new type errors)
- [x] `bun test` passes (200/200, 1 pre-existing skip)
- [ ] Verify downstream consumers (dsn-converter) build cleanly with updated package

title: Courtyards for more footprints
description: Adds courtyard rectangles to various component footprints to enhance PCB layout and design.

title: fix: correct SMC pad dimensions (pl/pw were swapped) to match KiCad
description: Fixes incorrect pad dimensions for the SMC (DO-214AB) footprint where pl and pw were swapped, correcting the pad length and width to match KiCad specifications.
body:
## Summary

- Fixes incorrect pad dimensions for the SMC (DO-214AB) footprint where `pl` and `pw` were swapped
- `pl` (pad length, horizontal) was 3.30mm but should be 2.50mm
- `pw` (pad width, vertical) was 2.50mm but should be 3.30mm
- Adds a KiCad parity test (`smc_kicad_parity.test.ts`) confirming exact match with `D_SMC` reference footprint
- Updates SVG snapshot to reflect the corrected geometry

## Before vs After

| Parameter | Before | After | KiCad D_SMC |
|--|--|--|--|
| pl (pad width) | 3.30mm | 2.50mm | 2.50mm |
| pw (pad height) | 2.50mm | 3.30mm | 3.30mm |
| p (spacing) | 6.80mm | 6.80mm | 6.80mm |

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test tests/smc.test.ts` passes with updated snapshot
- [x] `bun test tests/kicad-parity/smc_kicad_parity.test.ts` passes with 0% difference

Fixes #188

title: fix: correct SMA pad dimensions to match KiCad IPC-7351B standard
description: Fixes slightly incorrect pad dimensions for the SMA (DO-214AC) footprint by updating pad spacing and length to match the KiCad D_SMA reference footprint.
body:
## Summary

- Fixes slightly incorrect pad dimensions for the SMA (DO-214AC) footprint
- Updates pad spacing `p` from 4.05mm → 4.00mm and pad length `pl` from 2.45mm → 2.50mm to match the KiCad `D_SMA` reference footprint
- Adds a KiCad parity test (`sma_kicad_parity.test.ts`) with `avgRelDiff < 5%` assertion
- Updates the SVG snapshot

## Before vs After

| Parameter | Before | After | KiCad D_SMA |
|--|--|--|--|
| p (spacing) | 4.05mm | 4.00mm | 4.00mm |
| pl (pad length) | 2.45mm | 2.50mm | 2.50mm |
| pw (pad width) | 1.80mm | 1.80mm | 1.80mm |

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test tests/sma.test.ts` passes with updated snapshot
- [x] `bun test tests/kicad-parity/sma_kicad_parity.test.ts` passes with 0% difference

Fixes #188

title: fix: correct SOD-323F default width from '3,05mm' to '3.05mm'
description: Fixes the default value for the SOD-323F footprint body width from 3,05mm to 3.05mm to ensure correct rendering in circuit-json.
body:
## Summary

The `sod323f.ts` footprint has a typo in the default value for the `w` (body width) parameter: it uses a comma as the decimal separator (`"3,05mm"`) instead of a period (`"3.05mm"`).

`circuit-json`'s `length.parse("3,05mm")` returns `3` (truncating the decimal part), so the silkscreen outline is rendered with a body width of 3 mm instead of the correct 3.05 mm (SOD-323 standard body width).

## Fix

Change the default from `"3,05mm"` to `"3.05mm"` and update the SVG snapshot accordingly.

## Test plan
- [x] `bun test tests/sod323f.test.ts` passes after snapshot update
- [x] `bun run format` clean

title: fix: remove non-existent pcb_thtpad type from apply-origin filter
description: Removes invalid pcb_thtpad type from apply-origin filter to eliminate TypeScript error and ensure correct handling of THT pad bounds with pcb_plated_hole type.
body:
Fixes #501

The type `pcb_thtpad` does not exist in `circuit-json`'s `AnyCircuitElement` union — `pcb_plated_hole` is the correct type for through-hole pads. This removes the invalid `pcb_thtpad` comparisons from `apply-origin.ts`, eliminating the TypeScript error.

The `pcb_plated_hole` branch already handles THT pad bounds correctly.

Replaced the direct use of title and aria-label on the status indicator with a button wrapped in a tooltip, and adjusted the indicators opacity for the connected state for better visual feedback.
body: This pull request enhances the user experience for the API status indicator in the `RunFrameWithApi` component by adding a tooltip for accessibility and clarity. **UI/UX Improvements:** * Added a tooltip to the API status indicator using the `Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent`, and `TooltipProvider` components from `lib/components/ui/tooltip`, improving accessibility and providing a clearer status message on hover or focus. title: Updated the CLI documentation for `tsci` in the `docs/intro/installation.md`
description: Updates the CLI documentation for tsci to reflect a shift in terminology from snippets to packages and adds several new commands for improved user reference.
body:
This pull request updates the CLI documentation for `tsci` in the `docs/intro/installation.md` file, reflecting a shift in terminology from "snippets" to "packages" and adding several new commands. The changes improve clarity and provide a more comprehensive overview of the CLI's capabilities.

https://docs-ppdq2snn9-tscircuit.vercel.app/intro/installation

**CLI documentation updates:**

* Changed terminology throughout the help output and command list from "snippets" to "packages" to better reflect the current focus of the CLI. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e9475b40afcc1ea375e3b603281cb395ba4b74f1b59cc036f42a98d8f98d4b50L36-R66) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e9475b40afcc1ea375e3b603281cb395ba4b74f1b59cc036f42a98d8f98d4b50L60-R89)
* Added new commands to the CLI documentation, including `push`, `logout`, `build`, `transpile`, `add`, `remove`, `snapshot`, `setup`, `install`, `upgrade`, `doctor`, `check`, `registry`, `search`, `import`, `convert`, `simulate`, and `version`, providing a more complete reference for users. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e9475b40afcc1ea375e3b603281cb395ba4b74f1b59cc036f42a98d8f98d4b50L36-R66) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e9475b40afcc1ea375e3b603281cb395ba4b74f1b59cc036f42a98d8f98d4b50L60-R89)
* Updated descriptions for existing commands and options to match the new terminology and expanded functionality. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e9475b40afcc1ea375e3b603281cb395ba4b74f1b59cc036f42a98d8f98d4b50L36-R66) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e9475b40afcc1ea375e3b603281cb395ba4b74f1b59cc036f42a98d8f98d4b50L60-R89)

title: feat: add and enhance documentation for various `tsci` command-line tools.
description: This pull request significantly expands and standardizes the tscircuit CLI documentation. It adds full documentation for many previously undocumented commands, improves and clarifies options for existing commands, and enhances authentication and configuration docs. The update also documents new export and simulation formats and unifies formatting (usage, options, examples) across all CLI command pages for better clarity and consistency.
body:
This pull request significantly expands and standardizes the tscircuit CLI documentation. It adds full documentation for many previously undocumented commands, improves and clarifies options for existing commands, and enhances authentication and configuration docs. The update also documents new export and simulation formats and unifies formatting (usage, options, examples) across all CLI command pages for better clarity and consistency.

https://docs-pegcd6638-tscircuit.vercel.app/category/command-line-interface

title: Eliminate false DRC via-collision errors by carrying true routed via diameters through SRJ→circuit-json conversion
